title: OAL
description: Work on OpenAgentLayer source records, provider renderers, deployment, plugin payloads, manifests, and acceptance gates. Use for OAL-specific generator/deployer behavior.
body: This is OAL's index skill for AI/LLM use. Use it whenever work touches OpenAgentLayer behavior, source records, provider renderers, deploy/install flows, runtime hooks, plugin payloads, skills, agents, routes, manifests, versioning, or acceptance gates. OAL is source-driven: authored source is the product input and generated provider files are disposable outputs. Change `source/`, prompt templates, runtime hooks, renderers, deploy logic, package/plugin payloads, or acceptance checks so behavior is owned end to end; do not patch generated artifacts when an authored source exists. Read `references/surfaces.md` first when unsure which layer owns a behavior. AI agents should use OAL by selecting the route/skill that matches the task, inspecting controlling source, making the smallest complete source-backed change, and validating the rendered/provider behavior that proves it. Use provider-native agents when work can split by ownership, provider, package, route, test tier, research path, review perspective, or repeated batch item. Keep stable, slow-changing repo invariants in provider instruction files; keep fast-changing workflows in skills, hooks, commands, or rendered agents when the provider reload path supports them. In Codex, custom OAL agents are rendered in `.codex/config.toml` and `.codex/agents/*.toml`; Codex does not infer them automatically, so explicitly spawn rendered OAL agent names or aliases such as `hermes`, `hephaestus`, `atalanta`, `nemesis`, and `athena` when applicable, wait for their results, and merge only final summaries, changed paths, validation output, or precise blockers into the parent context. For significant or separable Codex coding implementation, prefer rendered GPT-5.3-Codex implementation workers such as `hephaestus`, `daedalus`, `demeter`, `hecate`, or `prometheus` instead of having the GPT-5.5 parent perform all edits. Use CSV/batch subagents for many similar row-shaped tasks when available. If native subagents are unavailable or the task needs external control planes, use `oal codex peer batch <task>`, `oal opendex`, `opendex`, or Symphony workflow commands for bounded orchestration. Keep narrow single-owner edits local and record the solo ownership reason.
